title: Ensure that the --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group argument is not set

input_row.title: Ensure that the --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group argument is not set
input_row.rationale: Setting the --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group flag circumvents the desired “approval” process. All the certificates are auto -approved without checking their integrity. This flag is meant to be used for development and testing purposes only and hence should not be used in the production.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-controller-manager Verify that the --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group argument is not set.
input_row.remediation: Edit the /etc/kubernetes/controller-manager file on the master node and remove the -- insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group argument from the KUBE_CONTROLLER_MANAGER_ARGS parameter. Based on your system, restart the kube-controller-manager service. For example: systemctl restart kube-controller-manager.service Impact: Invalid certificates will be rejected.
input_row.default_value: By default, --insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group is not set. 81 | P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kube_controller_manager_insecure_approve_csr_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-controller-manager -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'` to retrieve the arguments passed to the kube-controller-manager.
- Check the output for the presence of the `--insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group` argument.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The argument `--insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group` is NOT present in the output.
  - **Fail:** The argument `--insecure-experimental-approve-all-kubelet-csrs-for-group` is present in the output.
